name: Ants
blurb: Social hymenopterans nesting indoors and outdoors. Identification often hinges on petiole, antennal segments, and odor.

name: Cockroaches
blurb: Nocturnal scavengers found near food, moisture, and harborage. Pronotal markings and size separate the common indoor species.

name: Bed Bugs & Kin
blurb: Obligate blood-feeders associated with sleeping areas. Size, color, and harborage location guide field identification.

name: Stored-Product Pests
blurb: Beetles and moths that infest grains, flour, and dry goods. Larval damage is often the first signal.

name: Flies & Gnats
blurb: Drain, fruit, fungus, and house flies share habits but differ sharply in venation, size, and breeding substrate.

name: Spiders
blurb: Most indoor spiders are harmless. Two medically significant species in Indiana require careful, calm identification.

name: Wasps & Bees
blurb: Nest architecture and color pattern carry most of the diagnostic weight when specimens are not in hand.

name: Silverfish & Booklice
blurb: Small, soft-bodied pests of paper, starch, and damp materials. Often the first sign of moisture problems.
